data.title: Kit Email Marketing Tutorial: Step-by-Step for Creators
data.excerpt: Learn how to use Kit (ConvertKit) with this hands-on tutorial. Build automations, landing pages, and monetize your audience from day one.

$media.alt: Kit Email Marketing Tutorial: Step-by-Step for Creators

children.text: Kit Email Marketing Tutorial: A Complete Walkthrough for Creators

If you've been building an audience online, you already know the drill. Social platforms change algorithms overnight. Reach tanks without warning. But your email list? That's the one channel you actually own.

Kit (formerly ConvertKit) is the email marketing platform built specifically for creators. Not e-commerce brands. Not corporate newsletters. Creators—writers, podcasters, coaches, course builders, musicians, YouTubers. People who need to build genuine relationships with their audience and turn that connection into sustainable income.

I've been using Kit for over two years now, and the visual automations alone saved me hundreds of hours. Let me walk you through exactly how to set it up, step by step. By the end, you'll have a working system that grows your list and nurtures subscribers on autopilot.
*(Disclosure: Some links below are affiliate links. I only recommend tools I genuinely use and trust.)*
If you want the full breakdown of what this platform offers, check out
what Kit actually is
first.
Step 1: Create Your Kit Account
Head to
kit.com and start your free trial
. The free plan supports up to 1,000 subscribers with unlimited landing pages, forms, and email broadcasts. No credit card needed.
Here's what you'll do:
1. Click "Start free trial" on the homepage
2. Enter your name, email, and password
3. Answer a few quick questions about your audience size and content type
4. Verify your email
That's it. You're in.
The onboarding flow is clean. Kit doesn't bury you in settings menus. You land on a dashboard that shows your subscriber count, recent broadcasts, and a clear path forward.
Pro tip:
If you're migrating from another platform like Mailchimp or MailerLite, Kit offers free migration on paid plans. Their team handles the transfer so you don't lose subscribers or automations.
Step 2: Create Your First Landing Page
Before you send a single email, you need a place for people to subscribe. Kit's landing page builder is one of my favorite features—no external tools required.
Here's how to build one in under 10 minutes:
1. From your dashboard, click
Landing Pages & Forms
in the sidebar
2. Select
Landing Page
(choose "Form" for embeddable popups or inline forms)
3. Pick a template. Kit has dozens of clean, conversion-optimized designs
4. Customize the headline, description, and form fields
5. Connect it to a specific tag or sequence (more on this below)
6. Hit Publish
Kit gives you a unique URL for each landing page. Share it on social media, in your podcast show notes, or in your YouTube descriptions.
What I love: the pages load fast, look professional on mobile, and you can create as many as you want—even on the free plan.
Want to see all the ways creators use Kit?
Here's a deep dive on
Kit use cases
Step 3: Set Up Tags and Segments
Tags are how Kit organizes your audience. Think of them as labels that tell you what each subscriber cares about.
Examples:
`downloaded-free-guide`
`attended-webinar-march`
`interested-in-course`
`purchased-template-bundle`
To create tags:
1. Go to
Subscribers
in the top nav
2. Click
Create a Tag
3. Name it something descriptive
4. Apply it manually, through forms, or via automations
Segments let you group tags together. For instance, a segment called "Warm Leads" might include anyone tagged with `downloaded-free-guide` AND `opened-last-3-emails`.
This is where Kit shines compared to generic email tools. You're not just blasting everyone. You're sending the right message to the right people at the right time.
Step 4: Build a Visual Automation
This is the feature that made me switch to Kit permanently.
Visual Automations let you map out entire subscriber journeys as flowcharts. You see exactly what happens at every step—no guessing, no digging through nested menus.
Here's a simple welcome sequence automation:
1. Click
Automations
New Automation
2. Choose
Start from scratch
or pick a template
3. Set the
Trigger
: "Subscribes to a form" → select your landing page
4. Add a
Step
: "Email Sequence" → create a 3-5 email welcome series
5. Add
Delays
between emails (e.g., wait 1 day between each)
6. Optionally add
Conditions
: "If subscriber clicks link X, tag them as `interested-in-course`"
7. Add more steps—maybe send them to a different sequence based on behavior
8. Turn it on
Now every person who subscribes through that landing page automatically gets your welcome series. If they click a specific link, they get tagged and moved into a more targeted funnel.
All of this runs while you sleep. While you're recording your podcast. While you're at the park with your kids.
The paid Creator plan unlocks unlimited Visual Automations and email sequences
try it free for 14 days
. That's where the real magic happens.
Step 5: Write and Schedule Email Sequences
A sequence is a series of emails sent on a schedule. Perfect for welcome series, onboarding flows, or product launch funnels.
To create a sequence:
1. Go to
Sequences
New Sequence
2. Write your first email. Kit's editor is distraction-free—just you and your words
3. Set the send timing: Day 0 (immediately after trigger), Day 1, Day 3, etc.
4. Add more emails to the sequence
5. Hit
Save
and attach the sequence to an automation

Kit's editor supports markdown-style formatting, making it fast to write without wrestling with rich text formatting. You can add buttons, images, and customize the layout—but the default is clean and readable.
A/B testing tip:
On the Creator plan, you can A/B test subject lines to see what actually gets opens. Small tweaks like adding the subscriber's first name or asking a question can boost open rates by 15-20%.
For more optimization ideas, check out our
Kit tips and best practices
Step 6: Monetize Your List
Kit isn't just for sending newsletters. It's a monetization platform.
Built-in ways to earn:
Sell digital products directly
— PDFs, templates, presets, audio files. Upload, set a price, Kit handles delivery and payment via Stripe.
Offer paid subscriptions
— Turn your newsletter into a revenue stream. Gate premium content behind a monthly or yearly subscription.
Create a tip jar
— Let supporters pay what they want for your content.
Recommend other creators
— Kit's built-in recommendation engine lets you cross-promote newsletters and earn referral commissions.
You don't need Gumroad, Patreon, or a separate payment processor. Kit bundles everything.

I launched a $29 template bundle last month through Kit. The entire process—from product page to payment to automated delivery—took 20 minutes to set up. My subscribers got the product instantly, and I didn't touch a thing after clicking publish.
If you want to see why so many creators choose this platform, here's a breakdown of
why Kit stands out
New Features Kit Just Released (2026)
Kit keeps shipping updates that matter to creators. Here's what's new:
Kit MCP (Model Context Protocol)
— Connect Kit to AI tools and external apps for smarter workflows. Imagine automating personalized email drafts based on subscriber behavior data pulled from your CRM.
Enhanced Recommendation Engine
— Better cross-promotion mechanics so you grow faster through creator-to-creator recommendations.
Improved Visual Automation Builder
— More conditions, cleaner interface, faster load times.
Advanced Subscriber Scoring
— Find your highest-value subscribers automatically. Focus your energy on the people most likely to buy.
Kit announced these at their
Craft + Commerce 2026 event
, and they're already live on all plans.
Who Is Kit Ideal For?
Kit works best for creators who treat their email list as a core business asset—not an afterthought.
You'll love Kit if you're:
A newsletter writer
who wants clean, readable emails without design clutter
A podcaster or YouTuber
looking to convert listeners/viewers into email subscribers
A course creator or coach
who needs automated funnels that sell while you teach
An author
building a reader base before a book launch
A musician or artist
selling digital products directly to fans
A solopreneur
who wants one platform for email, landing pages, and payments
The free plan is genuinely generous—1,000 subscribers, unlimited broadcasts, unlimited landing pages. No weird feature gates that force you to upgrade before you're ready.
And when you do outgrow it, the Creator plan at $33/month (billed yearly) unlocks everything: unlimited automations, sequences, A/B testing, and Kit MCP.
Ready to Start? Here's Your Next Step
Stop collecting emails in a spreadsheet. Stop relying on platforms that don't belong to you.
Kit gives you the tools to build a real audience engine—one that grows your list, nurtures relationships, and generates income without you glued to a screen.
Start your free 14-day trial of Kit
.** No credit card. No commitment. Just you and a platform designed for creators who mean business.

children.text: Is Kit (ConvertKit) really free?

Yes. Kit's Newsletter plan is free for up to 1,000 subscribers. It includes unlimited landing pages, forms, email broadcasts, audience tagging, and even the ability to sell digital products. The free plan has limited automations, but it's more than enough to get started and validate your email strategy before upgrading.
How is Kit different from Mailchimp or MailerLite?

Kit is built specifically for creators—not businesses or e-commerce stores. The entire platform revolves around audience relationships, content-driven email, and creator monetization. Visual Automations, the recommendation engine, and built-in digital product sales set it apart from general-purpose email tools.
Can I sell products directly through Kit without a separate store?

Absolutely. Kit has native commerce features. You can sell digital downloads, offer paid newsletter subscriptions, and set up tip jars—all within Kit. Payments process through Stripe, and delivery is automatic. No need for Gumroad, Shopify, or Patreon.
